message:
perf(btree): quick_balance divider uses parse_leaf_table_rowid helper

Follow-up to bd-ah597 (b35f091c). `quick_balance_divider_rowid` runs on
the split hot path whenever a rightmost-leaf append overflows and
triggers the quick-balance short-circuit. It parsed the last LeafTable
cell via the generic `CellRef::parse` — decoding payload_size, rowid,
local_size, overflow bounds — and then read only `cell.rowid` from the
result. The cached-slot cost is also avoided here (the helper doesn't
touch CellSlotCache).

Switch to `CellRef::parse_leaf_table_rowid` (shipped in b35f091c), which
reads exactly two varints with one bounds check each.

Measured delta (same bench as bd-ah597):
  bench_parse_leaf_table_rowid_vs_full_parse (128 cells, 5M iters):
  rowid-only = 3.216 ns / call,  full parse = 6.474 ns / call
  -> 2.01x speedup on the parse step.

Every rightmost-append split that lands on the quick-balance path now
pays the cheaper parse. The `usable_size` parameter is still passed
through; dropping it would ripple through balance_quick's signature and
is out of scope for this drive-by.

Parity: cargo check -p fsqlite-btree clean; cargo clippy --lib --no-deps
        clean.
Bead:   bd-ah597 (this is a second caller of the same helper)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
